item.title: 乌克兰武装部队新任总司令在2014年与俄罗斯作战时赢得了声誉
item.description:
乌克兰基辅（美联社）——乌克兰军队的新任总司令在该国与俄罗斯的战争初期就树立了勇敢而坚定的战士的声誉，随后在执行了十年的战斗任务，同时稳步晋升。
基辅和其他城市发生大规模抗议活动后，总统弗拉基米尔·泽连斯基解雇了亚历山大·西尔斯基将军，周二，米哈伊洛·德拉帕蒂少将被任命为该职位。示威者要求解除他的职务，并恢复被视为军事现代化推动者的前国防部长米哈伊洛·费多罗夫的职务。抗议者称，西尔斯基体现了军队古老的苏联式指挥文化。
德拉帕蒂在脸书帖子中感谢西尔斯基为加强军队所做的工作。
“我是在其中长大的，”他指的是军队。“我将负责任地工作，专注并尊重今天保卫我们国家的人民。”
43 岁的德拉帕蒂是乌克兰新一代将军的代表，他们是在乌克兰与俄罗斯的斗争中磨炼出来的。他几乎整个职业生涯都在战斗岗位上度过，包括领导乌克兰联合部队，该部队指挥各军种的日常作战行动。
他以纪律严明、作战高效而闻名，经常被派去稳定前线最关键的地段。他很少在公开场合露面，但由于过去的胜利，他在军队内外赢得了广泛的尊重和声望。
他的声誉是在 2014 年俄罗斯在乌克兰东部战争初期建立的。那年 5 月，作为第 72 旅一个营的少校指挥，他率领一支装甲纵队进入马里乌波尔托中部，营救被亲俄武装分子围困的警察和国土防卫官员，这些武装分子威胁要活活烧死他们。
当纵队的道路被一米多高的路障挡住时，坐在领头车辆上的德拉帕蒂命令司机加速，车辆就像从坡道上跳下来一样弹过路障。这一时刻被广泛流传的视频所记录，被认为是早期抵抗运动中最引人注目的事件之一。
对于当时的乌克兰军队来说，这是一个大胆的举动，因为野战军官果断、独立的行动很少见。军队的装备仍然很差，基本上未经考验，并且对任何可能加剧与俄罗斯紧张关系的行动保持警惕。
几个月后，德拉帕蒂的营在俄罗斯边境附近被俄罗斯支持的部队包围了数周。德拉帕蒂并没有解除武装并走出去，而是选择浴血奋战，带领 260 名士兵和 30 多辆车辆在夜间逃出包围圈。
在接下来的十年里，他在战地指挥中不断晋升，领导乌克兰东部的旅，然后在 2022 年 2 月俄罗斯开始全面入侵时担任联合部队副司令。
2024年，他指挥部队阻止了俄罗斯向泽连斯基的家乡克里维里汉德的进攻，帮助将俄罗斯军队从赫尔松地区的部分地区击退，然后组织防御，削弱了俄罗斯2024年在哈尔科夫地区的新一轮进攻。
同年晚些时候，泽伦斯基任命他为地面部队司令。
但在俄罗斯对地面部队训练中心的一次袭击造成多名乌克兰士兵死亡后，他很快就辞去了这一职务，这是几个月来该设施发生的第二次此类袭击。他承担个人责任，称他无法确保他的命令得到执行。他在辞职报告中写道，官员们互相保护，阻止他改革该部门。
此后不久，泽连斯基任命他为联合部队司令。
周四，德拉帕蒂的不妥协态度被公开，前国防部长费多罗夫表示，乌克兰军方领导层抵制改革，并向推动改革的指挥官施压，其中包括德拉帕蒂，他说德拉帕蒂受到了西尔斯基的三次谴责。
德拉帕蒂在社交媒体上发帖回应，支持国防部的领导，但该帖子最终被驳回。他同意，许多变革是“尽管有系统，而不是由于它的运作方式”而推动的。他写道，指挥官的沉默“从来都不是一种责任”。
1982 年，他出生于乌克兰西部赫梅利尼茨基地区卡缅涅茨-波多利斯基的一个教师家庭。他在哈尔科夫坦克部队学院接受坦克军官培训，2004年毕业后被分配到白采尔科维的第72独立机械化旅，当时由西尔斯基指挥。

item.title: 卢比奥警告亚洲领导人，伊朗对霍尔木兹海峡的要求将开创危险的先例
item.description:
MANILA, Philippines (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Asian leaders Wednesday that Iran’s demand to control and collect tolls inthe Strait of Hormuzwould threaten the world’s economy and set a precedent that could be repeated elsewhere in the world.
Rubio spoke at the start ofan annual meetingin the Philippine capital, Manila, with counterparts from theAssociation of Southeast Asian Nationswho haveexpressed alarm over the resurgent warin the Middle East that has battered their region. Despite Washington’s preoccupation with the war, Rubio said “we’re with ASEAN 100%.”
“If we create a precedent in the Middle East where a nation state can decide that they are going to control an international waterway, charge a toll and if you don’t pay them, blow up your ships, we have created a very dangerous precedent which will repeat itself in other parts of the world, including in this region,” he said.
Washington’s top diplomat also renewed allegations that Iran has reneged on commitments under a failed peace deal. Rubio said Iran “has been reaching out to the United States, both directly and indirectly, to engage in talks about settling the differences,” but gave no indication that the U.S. was considering returning to peace talks with Iran.
Top diplomats from the U.S., China, Russia and several other countries are in Manila this week to take part in at the ASEAN ministerial gathering.Newly minted British Foreign Secretary Ed Milibandwas arriving Wednesday, his office said.
Sergey Lavrov, Russia’s foreign minister, told reporters that Moscow would like to see a quick settlement between the U.S. and Iran.
“There are those eager to incite each party to keep the conflict going, but we aren’t one of those,” he said.
“We would like to see it stop — it impacts the global economy and Russia is part of the global economy,” Lavrov said, adding that Moscow is “interested in stable global energy prices to pursue long-term economic policies.”
Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong underscored her country’s concern over the renewed war in the Middle East, warning that “the situation could deteriorate further without much warning.”
“We want to see an end to this war. We want to see a ceasefire. We want to see negotiations and we want the strait to be opened,” Wong told reporters.
“People are being affected by what is happening and has happened to global energy markets and the effect on people has been direct and personal,” she said.
Overreliance on fossil fuel imports from the Middle East has made Southeast Asia particularly vulnerable to the energy shock triggered by the Iran war, according to the International Energy Agency, which called the crisis a “stark wake-up call” for the region’s energy security.
Asian markets imported about 80% of the crude oil and almost 90% of the liquefied natural gas, or LNG, transiting through the Strait of Hormuz in 2025, the IEA said.
Rubio also met with counterparts in thefour-nation Quad partnership,which is made up of the U.S., Australia, India and Japan. They discussed maritime and transnational security, among other issues, and issued a joint statement after the talks that reaffirmed their commitment to maritime security initiatives aiming to counter China’s growing influence in the region.
During the meeting, Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi voiced strong opposition to any attempts to change the status quo by force in the region, including in the disputed South China Sea and East China Sea, where China’s increasingly aggressive actions have sparked concern.
Tokyo has a long-running dispute over Japan-controlled islands, which Beijing also claims, in the East China Sea.
Wong echoed his concern, saying “we have real concerns about behavior that is destabilizing and risky.”
The Trump administration has repeatedly criticized China’s assertive actions in the South China Sea to the disadvantage of smaller claimant countries like the Philippines, Washington’s oldest treaty ally in Asia.
The territorial disputes, which also involve Taiwan and ASEAN members Vietnam, Malaysia and Brunei, are high on the agenda of the talks.
ASEAN foreign ministers discussed the disputes on Tuesday, welcoming progress in yearslong negotiations between China and their regional bloc on a proposed non-aggression pact that aims to prevent a major conflict in the disputed waters that could draw in the United States.
A new altercation on Monday between Chinese and Filipino forces in a disputed shoal renewed concerns and prompted Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to summon the Chinese ambassador in Manila.
“We reaffirmed the need to enhance mutual trust and confidence, exercise self-restraint in the conduct of activities that would complicate or escalate disputes and affect peace and stability and avoid actions that may further complicate the situation,” the regional bloc’s ministers said in a draft joint statement.
Rubio said he will meet Russia’s foreign minister Lavrov on Thursday and discuss the war in Ukraine. He suggested he would also use the meeting to find other areas of potential cooperation between the U.S. and Russia.
He said the U.S. remains open to playing a role in ending the conflict “if that opportunity presents itself.”
Rubio said those efforts have “kind of fallen off a little bit over the last few months” but he will use the meeting with Lavrov to see if there’s an opportunity to renew talks.
Lavrov, meanwhile, said he will ask Rubio about U.S. President Donald Trump’s claim that a settlement on the Ukraine war is getting close.
Rubio also met with his Indian counterpart, External Affairs Minister Subrahmanyam Jaishankar, a U.S. official said. They discussed both regional issues and the Iran war, along with a yet-to-be completed trade agreement to lower Trump’s tariffs on Indian goods.
Rubio met separately with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi in a tightly secured convention complex by Manila Bay.
Associated Press journalists Haruka Nuga and Anton Delgado in Bangkok and Mari Yamaguchi in Tokyo contributed to this report.

item.title: 乌克兰总统泽伦斯基在抗议后解雇了军事首长并任命了继任者
item.description:
KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — PresidentVolodymyr Zelenskyyon Tuesday fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief ofUkraine’s armed forcesand named his replacement, following days of mass protests in Kyiv, the country’s capital, and other cities across the nation demanding his removal.
It was thesecond major changeZelenskyy has made under street pressure, after protests last summer forced him to undo a law weakening Ukraine’s anti-corruption watchdogs.
Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi asthe new army chief, according to a statement on social media. “Our shared wish is one — victory over the enemy and conditions at the front and in pressure on Russia that would force Russia toward peace,” Zelenskyy said.
The Ukrainian leader said he made the decision after a series of meetings with military commanders. They began shortly after protests broke out demanding the reinstatement of former Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, seen by demonstrators as a driver of military modernization, and the removal of Syrskyi, who they said embodied the army’s old, Soviet-style command culture.
Zelenskyy thanked Syrskyi for his achievements in Ukraine’s fight against Russia and praised him for the defense of Kyiv and for operations in Kharkiv and Kursk.
“I am grateful to Oleksandr Syrskyi and to every one of our warriors for Ukraine’s strong front-line positions,” he said. “I am grateful to Mykhailo Drapatyi for holding that same view.”
Drapatyi wrote in a Facebook post that serving Ukraine “has always been an honor for me, and during this war for independence, it means absolute responsibility.” He thanked Syrskyi for his consistent work strengthening the Ukrainian military.
“I grew up in it,” he added, referring to the army. “I will work responsibly, with focus and with respect for the people defending our state today.”
Zelenskyy said Drapatyi and other commanders must present an updated defense strategy, including continued reform of the corps system, faster weapons and drone deliveries, stronger air defense against Russian strikes and a clearer mobilization plan.
Zelenskyy met separately with Fedorov on Tuesday and offered him what he called a respected position in the government that would unify the country’s technology sector. It wasn’t clear if Fedorov accepted the offer — or whether the latest moves would satisfy protesters and calm the streets.
Fedorov congratulated Drapatyi, calling the appointment “new hope in the fight of free people for freedom and justice” and “a voice for change that could not go unheard.”
Fedorov had criticized Syrskyi publicly before his dismissal, saying the general was blocking his reform efforts and needed to be replaced for Ukraine not to fail in its resistance against Russia. He said Ukrainians’ high expectations must now be met with a clear vision for ending the war, decisive action to preserve soldiers’ lives, expand robotic warfare at the front, strike asymmetrically and exhaust Russia’s economy.
Fedorov called Syrskyi to thank him for defending the Kyiv region, the Kharkiv operation and other battles, calling it “already an important part of Ukraine’s history” but adding that the country must “move even faster and write new chapters, correcting all previous mistakes.”
The crisis began last Thursday, when Zelenskyy overhauled his wartime government, replacing his prime minister and firing Mykhailo Fedorov as defense minister after just six months in the post.
Zelenskyy cited an unworkable rift between Fedorov and Syrskyi, telling reporters that as the two couldn’t resolve their dispute, he would “have to resolve it.”
Following his dismissal, Fedorov publicly accused Syrskyi of blocking military reforms and fostering a top-down command culture, criticism that echoed complaints from soldiers and veterans over heavy casualties in costly front-line assaults.
The reshuffle immediately drew thousands of protesters into the streets of Kyiv and other cities, framing Fedorov’s removal as Zelenskyy siding with an entrenched military old guard over a reformer.
The backlash promptly spread to inside the military: the Air Force’s deputy commander resigned in protest, warning the move would weaken Ukraine’s air defenses; veterans and soldiers on leave joined the protests in Kyiv and other cities, and numerous public figures among soldiers spoke out publicly against Syrskyi on social media.
Syrskyi has long been associated with a Soviet-style, top-down command culture, and many protesters said they wanted a new generation of generals who would place a higher priority on preserving soldiers’ lives.
Within the military, Syrskyi has earned the gruesome nickname of “Butcher,” which particularly spread during his command of the defense of Bakhmut, one of the war’s longest and costliest battles.
His already precarious public standing eroded further after Ukrainian outlet Babel published an investigation in June detailing allegations of abuse within the Skelia assault regiment, a unit closely associated with Syrskyi that has reportedly suffered higher-than-average battlefield losses. The investigation documented dozens of deaths among recruits.

item.title: 美国军方确认了第三名据信在伊朗袭击中丧生的军人身份
item.description:
北卡罗来纳州罗利（美联社）——美国军方已证实，据信有第三名军人在周五约旦基地袭击事件中死亡，并确认他们是中士。Angel S. Rampersad，28 岁，纽约人。
这一确认使自战争开始以来阵亡的美国军人人数达到 18 人。
周一，五角大楼确认了来自夏威夷埃瓦海滩的 25 岁第一中尉泰勒·詹姆斯·费汉 (Tyler James Feehan) 和列兵的身份。19 岁的伊莎贝拉·冈萨雷斯 (Isabella Gonzales) 来自德克萨斯州卡罗尔顿，她是周五袭击中在约旦丧生的另外两名士兵。这些士兵被伊朗弹道导弹和无人机袭击身亡，这是自战争开始几天以来首次有士兵因伊朗直接火力死亡。美国中央司令部的一份声明称，他们被部署来支持美国打击伊斯兰国组织的任务。
袭击发生后，美国中央司令部宣布，两名军人死亡，另一人失踪。第二天，军事指挥部表示，部队发现了身份不明的遗骸，并正在对遗骸进行检查以核实身份。
现在，五角大楼表示，拉姆珀萨德被认为是失踪的士兵，她的官方身份现在是“职务状态 - 下落不明”，并且她“据信已经死亡”。
兰珀萨德与冈萨雷斯属于同一部队——总部位于德国安斯巴赫的第10陆军防空与导弹防御司令部。
此外，一名在伊拉克因伊朗无人机受控爆炸而丧生的美国士兵被确认为中士。据周二的一份军方声明及其家人称，来自北卡罗来纳州的 30 岁父亲迈克尔·伊曼纽尔·斯文顿 (Michael Emmanuel Swinton) 已服役近十年。
斯文顿将获得铜星勋章、紫心勋章和战斗行动徽章，并将晋升为上士。陆军表示正在调查他周日在埃尔比勒空军基地死亡的情况。
他的母亲说，他留下了一个 6 岁的儿子和一个 4 岁的女儿。
“我永远无法从这件事中恢复过来，”他的妻子米娅·冈萨雷斯-斯文顿周一在脸书上发帖称。“你回来后我们有很多计划，我都恶心了。”她说她周日接到通知说他已被杀。
上周，两名美国陆军士兵也在约旦防御伊朗弹道导弹和无人机袭击时丧生。这是自战争爆发以来第一批美军因伊朗直接炮火而死亡。
他的母亲克里斯特尔·斯温顿·安杜哈尔 (Christel Swinton Andujar) 告诉美联社，他的妻子和孩子是他世界的中心。
她通过短信说：“他的遗产将通过他感动的无数生命、他分享的笑声、他给予他人的力量以及他对家人倾注的无条件的爱而继续存在。”“他将永远被怀念，永远受到尊敬，永远是我们的英雄。”
斯文顿于 2017 年入伍，被分配到北卡罗来纳州布拉格堡的第 55 防空炮兵团、第 108 防空炮兵旅。
根据公开记录，他住在斯普林湖，这是布拉格堡家庭在基地外很受欢迎的地方。军方将他的家乡称为费耶特维尔，这是附近的另一个社区。
“斯温顿中士是一位经验丰富的士官，他对自己的工作和周围的人感到非常自豪，”准将说。约翰·L·道伯将军，陆军防空与导弹防御司令部司令。“他以勇气、荣誉和无私奉献响应了职责号召。”
美国和伊朗之间的重燃战火已进入第十天，双方都发动了接二连三的打击。伊朗袭击了美国在该地区的盟友，美国士兵驻扎在中东各地。
伊朗当局表示，美国在过去三周内发动的袭击已造成至少 50 人死亡、500 多人受伤。
双方都把目标瞄准了数百万人依赖的民用基础设施。船上的工人以及海湾国家、以色列和黎巴嫩的外国工人和其他人也在冲突中丧生。
美国国务院在向美国人发出的新警告中表示，“伊朗和支持伊朗的团体可能会瞄准美国海外的其他利益或与美国和全世界美国人有联系的地点。”
美国总统唐纳德·特朗普表示，为了阻止伊朗发展核武器，这场战争是必要的。共和党人特朗普现在面临着结束战争并避免他竞选时反对的那种长期中东冲突的政治压力。最近几周，局势升级推高了油价。
这个故事已被更正以显示中士。迈克尔·伊曼纽尔·斯文顿周日去世，而不是军方早些时候报道的周六。
柯林斯从康涅狄格州哈特福德报道。

item.title: 白宫承认使用关键词扼杀了加州价值数十亿美元的研究经费
item.description:
Federal agencies that terminated more than a thousand research grants at the University of California last year admitted that they used keywords related to diversity, gender, vaccine hesitancy and COVID-19 to screen for projects that ran afoul of the Trump administration’s priorities.
It’s an admission that lawyers for the research professors sayis proofthat the agencies illegally cancelled nearly $2 billion in grants. And that’s a major development in a lawsuit filed by UC researchers against the Trump administration to permanently get their grants back. The researchers’ legal team is now asking the judge in the case to skip a trial and formally declare that the White House repeatedly violated the constitution, including the First Amendment. A court hearing on whether that’ll happen is scheduled for Oct. 20.
The federal agencies made the admission about the keywords in signed stipulations filed in federal court last week in a case called Thakur v. Trump that’s been ongoingsince last spring.
The stipulations in the court filing show the agencies “did not terminate any grants based on alleged noncompliance with the terms of the grant” and instead used “general criteria, rather than grant-specific assessment of each award’s compliance, or performance,” the lawyers for the plaintiffs wrote. Lawyers for the UC professors now contend that the admission proves three wide-ranging constitutional violations:
1. By using keyword searches to review hundreds of grants and terminate them en masse rather than reviewing them individually on their merits, the Trump administration targeted research it found politically disfavorable, which is a First Amendment violation.
2. In terminating 283 Department of Energy grants from states where a majority voted for Kamala Harris in the 2024 election, and leaving untouched hundreds of grants that went to “red” states, the Trump administration wrongly punished researchers for merely living in states the White House politically opposed.
3. And by terminating billions of dollars in grants, and never rerouting that money to other grant opportunities, the Trump administration violated the constitution by not spending money in ways Congress specifically required through laws it passed on how research funds should be used.
CalMatters emailed the U.S. Department of Justice, which acts as the legal defense for the federal agencies, on July 16 to ask whether it agrees that the federal grant-making agency stipulations mean the Trump administration violated the constitutional rights of researchers. A spokesperson for the Justice Department didn’t respond by publication time.
The judge in this case, Rita F. Lin, has repeatedly sided with the UC professors and issued several preliminary injunctions forcing the federal agencies to restore the grants while she reviews the full merits of the case. This includes the restoration of hundreds of grants at UCLA that the Trump administration sought to suspendover its allegationsthat the university tolerated antisemitism and permitted transgender women to compete on women’s sports teams.
Those allegationswere also the subjectof a demand from the White House for UCLA to pay $1.2 billion or experience an additional cascade of research freezes. However, months before Trump sought that settlement UCLA took steps to address antisemitism on campus, including commissioning a task force to recommend ways to create a more welcoming environment for Jewish students.
Lin, a Biden appointee, faulted the administration for disregarding those efforts by UCLA in a preliminary injunction for a different but related lawsuit. The Trump administration’s justification for terminating the grants did not “mention the remedial steps UCLA had already taken to address the issues described,” Lin wrote.
Lawyers for professors in the Thakur case now want Lin to make a final ruling ordering the grants to be restored, rather than a preliminary one. Even if that were to happen, the federal agencies could decide to appeal Lin’s ruling to a higher court. The stakes “are huge,” lawyers for the professors in the Thakur case wrote. If the White House is “allowed to terminate this funding, the researchers, graduate students, and program staff that relied on the grants to pursue their lives’ work will all suffer significant damage to their reputations and careers.”
The lawyers warn of lifesaving cures that will never be developed, new understandings of diseases that will go unexplored, and the decline in the country’s global standing as an international juggernaut in publicly funded research.
The agencies admitted to using keywords in various ways, according to a CalMatters review of the new court filings.
The Department of Transportationidentifiedsix grants for projects worth about $42 million to terminate because they sought “transportation equity,” prioritized “disadvantaged communities,” or focused on “diversifying the transportation workforce” and “equity.”
The termination of three of those grants led by a UC Davis professor resulted in 77 researchers abandoning 79 in-progress projects. Lawyers for the plaintiffs wrote that the project leaders were also forced to lay off or scramble to replace funding for more than 40 graduate and undergraduate research assistants.
The National Institutes of Health admitted to using an internal search tool to flag UC grants that mentioned “health equity,” “work force diversity,” “structural racism,” and “sexual orientation.” The NIH alone suspended or cancelled more than 1,000 UC grants, including nearly 700 at UCLA, related to vaccines, cancer research and disparities in health outcomes. That figure is newly disclosed in a court filing — last year, UCLA indicated that closer to 500 NIH grants were terminated. The health research agency also declared that it’s possible that DOGE, theshort-livedfederal office that billionaire Elon Musk led in 2025, used AI to target grants for termination. Lawyers for the plaintiffs wrote to the judge that these admissions show the agencies “identified the viewpoints they wanted to suppress, searched the grants they funded for those that expressed those viewpoints, and terminated those grants on that basis.” That violates the researchers’ First Amendment rights, the lawyers wrote. The Environmental Protection Agency, one of the named agencies in the suit, isin the process of settlingwith lawyers for the UC professors to avoid further litigation.
A separate federal district judge in Massachusetts last weekdealt the Trump administration a blowin its ability to terminate grants and withhold federal funding. The White House relied on a legal phrase in many of its funding cancellations — “no longer effectuates . . . agency priorities.” California and 22 other states sued, arguing that there’s no law or right for the federal government to cancel funding based on new program goals and White House desires after the grants were already awarded. The judge agreed.
This story was originally published byCalMattersand distributed through a partnership with The Associated Press.

item.title: 特朗普宣布征收关税后，加拿大取消了与美国的联合开桥活动
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——在唐纳德·特朗普总统宣布对大多数加拿大商品征收 50% 的关税后，计划于周五举行的美加联合仪式庆祝连接底特律和安大略省温莎的戈迪·豪国际大桥开通，预计将不再举行。
在宣布关税时，特朗普指责加拿大不公平地歧视美国汽车、酒类和乳制品，加剧了与美国最亲密盟友之一的紧张关系。
加拿大基础设施部长格雷戈尔·罗伯逊 (Gregor Robertson) 发言人詹娜·加萨贝 (Jenna Ghassabeh) 在一份电子邮件声明中表示：“鉴于美国本周早些时候威胁采取贸易行动，两国之间继续举办庆祝活动是不合适的。”
在此之前，美国和加拿大官员本月早些时候就开放戈迪豪国际大桥达成协议，此前两国之间的分歧导致戈迪豪国际大桥的开通。
加拿大现在计划周五举行自己的仪式。目前尚不清楚美国是否会单独举行仪式。
两名不愿透露姓名的美国官员表示，他们仍预计大桥将于 7 月 27 日通车，但与之前相比，他们的确定性有所下降。
加萨贝表示：“我们仍然致力于在 7 月 27 日开通大桥，并在 7 月 24 日与加拿大人一起庆祝这一里程碑。”
这座长 1.5 英里（2.4 公里）的桥梁横跨底特律河，连接底特律和温莎。剪彩仪式原定于 6 月 12 日举行，但在官员表示美国和加拿大仍在努力解决“悬而未决的问题”后突然推迟。
加拿大根据一项协议资助了这座桥的建设，允许其通过通行费收入收回成本。特朗普政府曾寻求改变这一安排。
该项目由前密歇根州州长里克·斯奈德 (Rick Snyder) 谈判达成，自 2018 年起一直在建设中，耗资近 44 亿美元。
这座桥以加拿大冰球巨星戈迪·豪 (Gordie Howe) 的名字命名，他曾在底特律红翼队 (Detroit Red Wings) 度过了 25 个赛季。预计将成为加拿大与美国之间的又一条经济大动脉。
今年二月，特朗普在社交媒体上发帖要求加拿大给予美国政府至少一半的大桥所有权，并同意其他未具体说明的要求，这是他与加拿大在贸易问题上发生更广泛冲突的一部分。
这些紧张局势本周再次出现。白宫表示，关税将在 30 天内生效，为谈判留出时间。特朗普此前曾推迟或放弃了一些受到威胁的进口税。
加拿大总理马克·卡尼在一份关于关税的声明中表示，加拿大政府相信“自由和公平贸易的好处”，并仍准备与特朗普政府进行谈判。
随着加拿大考虑如何应对，关税可能升级为更广泛的贸易战。
安大略省省长道格·福特在社交媒体上发帖称：“如果这些关税继续下去，加拿大应该以一美元一美元的价格回应关税。”

item.title: 特朗普政府表示将推迟向加州和明尼苏达州支付 10 亿美元医疗补助
item.description:
The Trump administration on Tuesday said it was deferring more than $1 billion in Medicaid payments to Minnesota and California because of “suspected fraud and noncompliance,” the latest ina series of punitive stepsit has linked toallegations of fraudinmostly Democratic-led states.
Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said the new actions — which come after previously announced Medicaid funding deferrals in those states — are part of the administration’s strategy to “stop the fraud before it happens” rather than claw back problematic spending after bad actors are prosecuted, as previous administrations had done.
“We have a duty to stop the payments, demand answers and then follow the evidence wherever it leads,” Kennedy told a news conference.
The tactic is part of the administration’s sweeping campaign to show it’s cracking down on fraud and saving taxpayers money when rising healthcare costs and other economic stressors have made affordability a top voter concern in November’s midterm elections.
In March, Vice President JD Vance launched a new anti-fraud task force at the request of President Donald Trump, bringing together officials from various departments to use data and technology to identify and investigate suspected wrongful use of federal dollars.
But the federal government hasn’t provided “data or explanation on how the deferral amount was calculated or what it was based on” to Minnesota, said John Connolly, the temporary commissioner and state Medicaid director for Minnesota’s Department of Human Services.
“Today’s actions show that the federal government is acting again in unprecedented and punitive ways as part of their war on Medicaid and its recipients,” he said in a statement. “Partnership — not politics — is required to stop criminals and protect services for the people who need them.”
Minnesota’s Democratic Gov. Tim Walz suggested the Republican administration was deferring the funds to pay for Trump’s tax cuts to wealthy Americans. California’s Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom accused the administration of targeting it for political reasons.
The Trump administration intensified anti-immigration efforts in Minnesota earlier this year, sending in a robust force of Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers whose actions there caused weeks of counterprotests and included the shooting deaths of two civilians. Trump himself has frequently denounced California as a badly governed state.
The strategy has led to some false starts and headaches for states.
In April, for example, the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services acknowledged to The Associated Press itmade a significant errorin figures it used to help justify a fraud probe in New York. Last month, California’s Medicaid director told a congressional committee that CMS had not yet provided “any instances of fraud, waste or abuse” to the state in its justification of a $1.3 billion Medicaid funding deferral announced in May.
And in Minnesota, state officials have been executing a thorough corrective action plan to defray CMS concerns that have led the agency to defer some $260 million in federal money and to threaten future cuts.
“We have cooperated in good faith and proactively engaged the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services to first raise the alarm on fraud in Minnesota’s Medicaid program, to effectively investigate fraud and to institute further safeguards against future misuse of funds,” Connolly said.
Kennedy didn’t specify in Tuesday’s announcement whether the new deferrals — $867.5 million in federal Medicaid payments to California and $199 million to Minnesota — are in addition to, or overlap with,earlier deferralsannounced this year.
CMS Administrator Dr. Mehmet Oz didn’t provide concrete examples of fraud in the two states in justifying the deferrals. He did mention some patterns the agency noticed that it found questionable, including that some providers were billing for four or more patients at the same time, or billing after the date of a Medicaid beneficiary’s death.
Oz also highlighted a fast rate of growth in California’s home care program as a reason for concern. California officials have disputed that idea, explaining that the state’s home care program has grown because of an intentional strategy to keep people out of more expensive nursing homes.
“In-home care growth reflects intentional, federally encouraged expansion, not improper spending,” said Anthony Cava, spokesperson for the California Department of Health Care Services. “California is calling on CMS to stop threatening care for California’s most vulnerable residents.”
Kennedy and Oz said states could restart the flow of federal funding by providing documentation proving the payments in question were legitimate.
Officials in both states have previously acknowledged they are working with the federal government to provide the requested information. Oz said Minnesota had already returned documents to the federal government and they were being “reevaluated very carefully.”
Kennedy also suggested Tuesday that he would extend the power to exclude providers from Medicaid, Medicare and other federal health programs to CMS. In the past, that authority has rested solely with his department’s Office of the Inspector General.
“This is going to be a full force multiplier,” HHS Inspector General Thomas March Bell told a news conference. “It’s going to create additional momentum, and it’s going to exclude additional bad actors.”

item.title: 上诉法院驳回拜登阻止发布代笔采访录音的请求
item.description:
华盛顿（美联社）——联邦上诉法院驳回了乔·拜登提出的阻止公开他当选总统前回忆录代笔人采访录音和笔录的请求。
美国哥伦比亚特区巡回上诉法院由三名法官组成的分歧小组将其决定推迟到 8 月 3 日，以便让拜登有更多时间考虑再次上诉。
该小组周一晚间以 2 比 1 的裁决发现，披露拜登希望保密的材料符合“重大”公众利益。大多数人指出，对录音进行编辑将有助于保护拜登的隐私。
裁决称：“我们的结论是，因披露现已编辑的材料而对个人隐私造成的任何剩余侵犯可能不会超过披露的公众利益。”
美联社记者哈亚·潘瓦尼 (Haya Panjwani) 报道了乔·拜登在司法部调查中的上诉。
由民主党人拜登提名的法官弗洛伦斯·潘(Florence Pan)撰写了一份反对意见，她在其中表示，拜登在隐藏这些材料方面表现出了“巨大的隐私利益”。
她写道：“有争议的谈话发生在拜登的家中，政府在刑事调查过程中获得了录音，但并未导致起诉。”
华盛顿特区巡回法院首席法官 Sri Srinivasan 和法官 Gregory Katsas 也听取了上诉。斯里尼瓦桑由民主党总统巴拉克·奥巴马提名，并选择拜登作为副总统。卡萨斯是由共和党总统唐纳德·特朗普提名的，他在 2020 年总统选举中输给了拜登，但在 2024 年再次当选入主白宫。
马克·兹沃尼策 (Mark Zwonitzer) 曾与拜登合作撰写两本回忆录，分别是 2007 年的《要兑现的承诺》和 2017 年的《爸爸，答应我》。他于 2016 年和 2017 年在拜登家中采访了拜登。拜登的律师表示，这些谈话是坦诚的、私人的，并且旨在保密。
拜登发言人 T.J.达克洛表示，前总统不同意这一裁决，但“尊重法院以及独立司法机构在健康民主中发挥的重要作用。”
达克洛在一份声明中说：“十年前，拜登总统在一本书中讨论了他已故儿子的谈话，这些谈话是私人的，并已提供给司法部，但条件是保持这种状态。”“扭转方针并将其公开只是本届政府将（司法部）武器化以进行政治报复的最新例子。”
这些录音是由特别检察官罗伯特·胡尔获得的，他调查了拜登在担任特拉华州参议员和奥巴马副总统期间处理机密文件的情况。在胡尔拒绝对时任总统提出指控后，国会共和党人要求提供这些材料。
拜登提起诉讼，寻求禁令，阻止特朗普领导下的司法部向国会和保守派传统基金会公布这些录音。该部门此前辩称，根据公共记录法，这些录音可以免于披露。
特朗普提名的美国地区法官达布尼·弗里德里希 (Dabney Friedrich) 在 6 月份裁定，公众对这些材料的兴趣超过了拜登所拥有的任何隐私权，之后拜登提出上诉。
潘指出，多数人实际上是在裁定，拒绝拜登在上诉期间发出禁令的请求，支持立即披露这些材料。
“当然，这对这个案子来说是有争议的，”她写道。

item.title: 赫格斯在激烈的听证会上为资金请求辩护，估计伊朗战争已造成 375 亿美元的损失
item.description:
WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense SecretaryPete Hegsethfaced fiery questions and protests Tuesday over theU.S. war with Iran, which he estimated has cost $37.5 billion so far, as Republicans prepare a$95 billion packageto fund the military, along with other White House priorities.
Hegseth provided the new estimate, up from earlier assessments, during a combative Senate Appropriations Committee hearing, which came days after the Pentagon announced that three moreAmerican service membershave died in the conflict, bringing thedeath toll to 17, and that more than 100 have been injured since early July. Several times protesters interrupted his opening remarks.
Hegseth said the supplemental war funding is an “urgent, necessary” injection of money, and withPresident Donald Trump’sproposed$1.5 trillion defense budgeta generational investment in the military.
“Without these funds, we face critical shortfalls,” Hegseth testified.
But Sen. Patty Murray, the top Democrat on the panel, warned of “another forever war.”
“This war is now spiraling out of control again,” Murray said.
PresidentDonald Trumphas said repeatedly that “a deal is near, and the war will be over soon,” she said. “But now, he’s asking for $70 billion more — and for us to just trust him it’ll all work out fine.”
Gen. Dan Caine, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, and Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins were also testifying before the panel. The proposed budget package includes $60 billion and $12 billion for other national security needs, along with $10 billion for farm aid and $10 billion for voting law changes.
Before the hearing, the White House issued a statement urging support of the budget resolution, “without modification — immediately.”
War funding makes up the bulk of the GOP’s new budget proposal, one last priority package the Republicans hope to launch before lawmakers recess to campaign for themidterm elections. The package aims to help farmers struggling under Trump’s tariffs and to pay for voter law changes, none of it paid for with offsets.
Most Americans continue to disapprove of how Trump is handling Iran,according to an AP-NORC poll.Gas prices have spiked as Iran works to control oil shipments through theStrait of Hormuz, U.S. munitions are running low from the onslaught of American missile strikes and Trump has shifted away from his own campaign promise not to draw the U.S. into prolonged military conflicts.
Nevertheless, Trump has showed no signs of backing off the war, and the Republicans have largely refused to confront his strategy or use Congress’ power of the purse and war powers authority to halt the conflict.
“The commander in chief and the whole Department of War are working very hard to bring this to a resolution,” said HouseSpeaker Mike Johnsonat his weekly press conference Tuesday.
Hegseth appeared before the panel facing a torrent of questions about not only the war, but his own leadership of the department.
Sen. Susan Collins, the Republican chair of the committee, opened the hearing saying it’s “deeply troubling” that the Pentagon has blocked or denied promotions to various officers, particularly women and minorities.
Democratic Sen. Jeanne Shaheen of New Hampshire questioned why Hegseth is asking for more money when the department has not yet spent about half the $150 billion Republicans provided the Pentagon in last year’s big budget bill.
“Why are you asking the American people to absorb the cost of a war they don’t support?” asked Shaheen.
When Hegseth started describing the “historic nature” of Trump’s military strategy, she cut him off. Hegseth later explained that the rest of last year’s budget package would be spent by September.
When GOP Sen. Mike Rounds wanted to know if this funding request would be enough to “get the job done,” the general demurred.
“I can’t answer the question on what it will cost, because the enemy has a vote,” Caine said.
Republican Sen. John Kennedy of Louisiana was among several senators demanding answers over the situation in the Strait of Hormuz, asking if Iran will impose tolls on what historically has been an open waterway vital to the flow of oil.
Caine said, “That’s a hypothetical question, and I don’t know the answer to that.”
But Kennedy retorted: “Guys, we need straight answers.”
Tensions exploded toward the end of the more than three-hour hearing.
Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand, D-N.Y., told Hegseth flatly that his war claims don’t add up.
Has the U.S. obliterated Iran’s nuclear capabilities or not? she demanded to know.
“We’re staying laser-focused on the objective,” Hegseth responded, “and you’re trying to make campaign commercials.”
Sen. Jon Ossoff, D-Ga., probed Hegseth’s past assessments of the war, including the secretary’s claims of Iran’s surrender.
When Democratic Sen. Gary Peters of Michigan said the administration’s war strategy has been a complete “failure,” Hegseth erupted.
“Shame on you and others who call this a quagmire and a failure,” Hegseth said.
Peters told the secretary: “You, sir, are the failure.”
The senator said it’s not the U.S. troops who are failing, but their leaders. “You don’t have a strategy,” Peters said. “You don’t have a long-term plan to actually win this war.”
Hegseth said Democrats have “Trump Derangement Syndrome” and won’t give him the money he needs.
Peters said it’s not about money, “but a question of leadership.”
This week, the Senate will consider anotherwar powers resolution, having voted nearly a dozen times to try to halt the conflict.
The GOP budget proposal is expected to flow through the reconciliation process, which enables the majority to approve the plan on its own, over the objections of Democrats in the minority.
It’s the same lengthy process Republicans used to approve Trump’s big tax cuts bill last summer, as well as Homeland Security money earlier this year.
The proposal is headed toward a House vote this week, which would start the process by instructing various committees to begin drafting the legislation. Johnson can afford only a few defectors from his slim majority, and some conservatives want the funding offset with spending reductions elsewhere.
GOP senators have so far panned the House budget plan, but are waiting to see if Johnson can kickstart the process.
Associated Press writers Matt Brown and Ben Finley contributed to this report.

item.title: 备忘录称 ESPN 因收购 NFL Network 而开始裁员
item.description:
ESPN 自 4 月份接管 NFL Network 以来，三年来首次进行大规模裁员。
美联社获得的董事长吉米·皮塔罗 (Jimmy Pitaro) 在周二上午给员工的一份备忘录中表示，大多数举措都是 ESPN 去年收购 NFL Network 和其他联盟数字资产的结果。
该交易于去年 8 月宣布，还包括 NFL Fantasy 以及向有线和卫星运营商分发 RedZone 频道的权利。作为回报，NFL获得了ESPN 10%的股权。
一月份它获得了政府监管机构的批准。NFL Network 员工于 4 月 1 日成为 ESPN 的一部分。
皮塔罗在备忘录中写道：“在过去的几个月里，我们在将收购的 NFL 资产整合到 ESPN 方面取得了重大进展。”“在整个过程中，我们花时间仔细评估我们的集体团队、资源和组织结构，以便为我们的未来做好最佳定位。因此，我们不得不就我们今天将要传达的工作影响做出一些艰难的决定。
“虽然大部分工作影响与收购有关，但我们今天还将通知公司其他部门的同事，他们的职位受到了影响。我们致力于以同情心和尊重的态度对待员工，并在他们度过这一转变过程中提供支持。”
西雅图海鹰队主教练迈克·麦克唐纳（中）于 2026 年 2 月 26 日在印第安纳波利斯举行的 NFL Network 节目中发表讲话。（美联社照片/Julio Cortez，档案）
虽然大部分裁员影响的是制作和幕后人员，但一些播音人才也受到了影响。瑞安·克拉克 (Ryan Clark) 于 2015 年加入 ESPN，担任 NFL 分析师，他于周一在《NFL Live》节目中获悉自己被解雇的消息。一位知情人士表示，克拉克最初是在周二早上收到有关这一决定的报道之前被告知的。
克拉克是公司高管告诉他的，而不是从其他地方听到的。他没有完成在节目中的亮相。
该人士不愿透露姓名，因为他们无权讨论人事决定。
克拉克周二在社交媒体上讨论了裁员问题。他还分享了一段自己锻炼的视频。
“向今天被 ESPN 解雇的所有人员致以祈祷和关爱。你们中的许多人都将自己的生命倾注在这家公司中，我知道你们现在的感受，”克拉克在 X 上写道。“我的希望是，当这扇门关闭时，另一扇门会为你们所有人打开。愿上帝保佑！”
其他著名人物包括卡尔·拉维奇 (Karl Ravech)，他自 1993 年起就加入 ESPN，并成为其棒球报道中不可或缺的一部分。拉维奇是 2022-25 年《周日棒球之夜》的配音员，本赛季该节目转至 NBC 播出。他确实主持了周一晚上洛杉矶道奇队和费城费城人队之间的比赛。
自 2017 年以来一直在 NFL Network 工作的汤姆·佩利塞罗 (Tom Pelissero) 也在裁员之列。ESPN 最近与 NFL 内部人士伊恩·拉波波特 (Ian Rapoport) 和亚当·谢夫特 (Adam Schefter) 续约。
ESPN 的裁员也是华特迪士尼公司本周进行的其他裁员的一部分。

item.title: 世界杯决赛输给西班牙后，梅西返回阿根廷休息
item.description:
阿根廷罗萨里奥（美联社）——在世界杯决赛输给西班牙后，莱昂内尔·梅西于周二返回阿根廷休息几天。
梅西的私人飞机于早上6点27分从佛罗里达州劳德代尔堡降落在他的家乡罗萨里奥附近。尽管许多球迷举着标语和旗帜等待，但他并没有公开露面。
他在自己的 Instagram 账户上发帖称：“痛苦是巨大的，伤口需要时间才能愈合。”“我珍惜所有美好的时刻和将永远持续的记忆，以及整个国家的支持，再加上这个团队的辛勤工作和努力，推动我们再次跻身世界最佳之列。”
支持者们只瞥见了在停机坪上与梅西会面的SUV，将他和他的家人送往布宜诺斯艾利斯以北约300公里（185英里）的富内斯私人社区。
据报道，这位阿根廷队长与他的妻子安东内拉·罗库佐和三个孩子一起旅行，他们都陪伴他参加了整个北美比赛。在罗萨里奥等待他的是他 68 岁的父亲豪尔赫，他的家人在世界杯期间透露了他正在接受一种未公开的疾病的治疗。
凌晨3点30分，粉丝们就聚集在机场，希望一睹这位明星的风采。其中有两名身着Albiceleste球衣的年轻女孩举着欢迎牌，其中一张牌上写着：“梅西：你是一个传奇。感谢你给我们带来欢乐。整个国家都爱你！”
梅西预计将在七月底或八月初重新加入迈阿密国际队，参加正在进行的美国职业足球大联盟(MLS)赛季
阿根廷世界杯 26 名球员名单中的 15 名成员在主教练莱昂内尔·斯卡洛尼 (Lionel Scaloni) 的带领下于周一乘坐包机抵达布宜诺斯艾利斯埃塞萨机场。
周日，在新泽西州东卢瑟福举行的世界杯决赛中，西班牙队凭借费兰·托雷斯的进球以 1-0 输给了西班牙队。
斯卡罗尼于周二抵达他位于圣达菲省小镇普哈托的家，这是一件大事，他的邻居们聚集在一起欢迎他。
几分钟后，他走出家门，开始在人群递给他的阿根廷球衣上签名，并摆好姿势拍照。
“我们没有获胜，但我们可以感到满意。无法带来那种快乐是很痛苦的，”斯卡罗尼告诉记者。“我们输了，因为他们更好……也许我们已经筋疲力尽了。承认这一点很好——这会让我们变得更好。”
斯卡洛尼表示，他将履行与阿根廷足协 (AFA) 的合同，直至 12 月 31 日，但他没有确认是否会在该日期之后继续效力。“我会在这里待到十二月，伙计们……我们不要大惊小怪，”他补充道。
周日，梅西继 2014 年和 2022 年之后第三次闯入世界杯决赛。他以 8 个进球获得金靴亚军，落后法国队的基利安·姆巴佩 (Kylian Mbappé) 两个进球。
姆巴佩以 22 球高居世界杯历史进球榜榜首，比梅西多 1 球。
“现在很难完全欣赏我们的成就，但是这支球队连续两届进入世界杯决赛。我从心底感谢大家的每一条问候和信息，”梅西补充道。“我还要祝贺西班牙赢得冠军。”

item.title: 安全运动中心将开始公布其禁止名单上的人员姓名
item.description:
美国安全运动中心周二开始公布其纪律数据库中的人员名单，此举旨在提高透明度，并让人们更多地关注该中心认为是其打击性虐待的主要工具之一。
该中心负责打击奥林匹克运动中的虐待行为，长期以来一直发布所谓的集中纪律数据库。但在不公开名单上的人的情况下，人们必须访问数据库并知道他们在寻找谁，以了解他们周围被禁止和限制的人。
现在，该中心将每周分享有关添加到列表中的人员的最新信息；更新内容将包括已提交仲裁并已作出决定的案件。
该中心首席执行官贝尼塔·菲茨杰拉德·莫斯利 (Benita Fitzgerald Mosley) 表示：“知识就是力量，当信息尽可能容易获取时，维护安全的运动环境就会得到改善。”“通过分享已添加到集中纪律数据库的个人的每周新闻更新，我们希望通过提高公众意识来改善保护。”
收到该中心信件的包括国家管理机构、美国奥林匹克和残奥委员会以及媒体，这使得某人被列入数据库的消息更有可能被看到。
公开违规者是反兴奋剂的核心原则。公开宣布服用兴奋剂的人被认为是对吸毒的一种威慑，这种做法是世界禁毒规则的一部分。
安全体育中心在宣布这一变化的新闻稿中表示，数据库中列出的人员目前受到限制或禁止参加奥运会或残奥会运动——这一处罚涵盖了从精英到基层的各个阶层。该中心表示，该数据库是一个及时的快照，即名单上的某人可能会从其中消失，并且该中心的程序既不是刑事诉讼也不是民事诉讼。

item.title: 《哥斯拉》聋哑演员凯莉·霍特尔 (Kaylee Hottle) 18 岁时因车祸身亡
item.description:
弗雷德里克县治安官办公室称，因出演两部《哥斯拉》电影而闻名的聋哑演员凯莉·霍特尔周二凌晨在马里兰州的一场车祸中丧生。她18岁。
凌晨 3 点前，汽车冲出道路并撞上伊贾姆斯维尔的一个涵洞，霍特尔是车上两名乘客之一。据治安官办公室发布的新闻稿称，司机被送往当地一家医院，伤势没有生命危险，另一名乘客拒绝接受治疗。治安官办公室表示，据信超速是造成事故的一个因素，目前事故正在调查中。
霍特尔是美国手语的母语者，她的家人也是聋人。她的父母约书亚·霍特尔 (Joshua Hottle) 和凯西·霍特尔 (Ketsi Hottle) 在社交媒体网站上分享了有关她死亡的感人视频声明。
“凯莉的妈妈和四个兄弟姐妹都崩溃了，”她的父亲在机场发的一篇帖子中用美国手语说道。“他们都在家里，悲伤不已。我正在前往马里兰州的路上，将凯莉的遗体带回家。”
她的父亲说，霍特尔在从事故现场乘飞机抵达医院之前，她的心脏停止了跳动。“非常感谢我们一起度过的 18 年，并希望我们能拥有更多，”他说。
约书亚·霍特尔 (Joshua Hottle) 也联系了司机，治安官办公室称司机是一名 19 岁的弗雷德里克男子。
“我已经原谅你了，”她的父亲说。“别让这件事毁了你的余生。”
“我仍然觉得凯莉和我们在一起，”她的母亲在一篇帖子中用美国手语说道。
在《哥斯拉大战金刚》和续集《哥斯拉x金刚：新帝国》中，霍特尔扮演了一个名叫贾的孤儿女孩，她看穿了金刚威胁性的外表，并与怪物建立了特殊的联系，并用手语与它交流。
“听到这个消息我非常伤心。你会深深地想念凯莉，”她在《哥斯拉》中的搭档米莉·鲍比·布朗在 Instagram 上发帖说。
联合主演亚历山大·斯卡斯加德 (Alexander Skarsgård) 学习美国手语是为了与霍特尔交流，他在 2021 年告诉流行文化网站 Junkee，这位年轻天真的专业精神以及她快速将导演的笔记融入表演的能力给他和其他人留下了深刻的印象。
在 2024 年接受美联社采访时，霍特尔通过翻译表示，她在出演《哥斯拉》之前从未看过怪物电影，因此她的父亲帮助她了解了这一类型。
“我尊敬很多人，但我尊敬的一个人，我最尊敬的少数人，是我的父母和姐妹，”霍特尔说。
霍特尔说，当她拍摄第一部电影时，她有点担心之后是否能够恢复“正常生活”。
“人们确实在学校认出了我，是的，但这并不像我想象的那么糟糕，”霍特尔说。
霍特尔此前曾出演过 2021 年电视剧集《Magnum P.I.》她被列为出演即将上映的电影“什么不会杀死我们”。
“我非常心碎。在这个难以想象的时刻，我的心与她的家人同在，”她在这部电影中的联合主演、聋人演员阿拉夸·考克斯在 Instagram 上写道。
其他著名的聋人演员也在网上分享了悲伤的信息。
“我对可爱的凯莉·霍特尔的去世感到非常难过。愿她的美丽和才华成为永恒的记忆，”奥斯卡奖得主玛莉·马特林在 Instagram 上写道。
主演电影《尾声》的奥斯卡奖得主特洛伊·科瑟写道，霍特尔的去世让他想起了自己的女儿，并提醒他不要把时间视为理所当然。
科楚尔写道：“对于今天早上发生的事情，我无话可说。”他向霍特尔一家表示“最深切的哀悼”。
奥斯汀德克萨斯聋人学校的官员也在脸书上宣布了霍特尔的去世，并写道，她的同学和朋友将获得咨询和支持服务。
学校写道：“当我们共同哀悼这一巨大损失时，请惦念凯莉的亲人。”___ 布恩从爱达荷州博伊西报道。拉格从密歇根州安娜堡报道。

item.title: 电影评论：匹克球是体育喜剧《丁克人》的明星
item.description:
《丁克人》是《躲避球：一个真正的失败者故事》的精神后裔，讲述的是网球团体在一个紧张的俱乐部里对抗匹克球爱好者的故事。这个故事本身非常符合书本：一位曾经前途无量的网球神童学会了克服自己和他对“次要运动”的蔑视。
在世界的一个版本中，它可能是“前沿”。但《丁克人》选择了接地气、傻乎乎的路线，有着令人难忘的古怪角色和一些高调的客串，这些都构成了一部令人愉快的观看。
杰克·约翰逊饰演达斯蒂，他专横的父亲查克（艾德·哈里斯饰）从他出生起就一直在培养他成为网球明星。在一段有趣的介绍中，我们得知，早在他告诉儿子自己的真名之前，他就称呼他的儿子为“锤子”。但与许多体育神童一样，达斯蒂很早就熄火了（在一场与年轻的安迪·罗迪克的比赛中，同样如此）。现在，他在父亲的俱乐部里给孩子们教网球课，并给他们讲述他辉煌岁月的故事，让他们感到无聊。他只剩下一个朋友：PJ，一位由澳大利亚喜剧演员陈亚伦饰演的热心阿谀奉承、心地善良的怪人。
达斯蒂和他的父亲非常讨厌匹克球，所以当达斯蒂的医生（由本·斯蒂勒担任制作人的简短而有趣的出场）建议将其作为手腕受伤的康复治疗时，这是一个比死亡更糟糕的判决。当然，当他开始与一群年长的女性一起演奏时，包括坎迪斯（玛丽·斯汀伯根饰）、盖尔（克莱奥·金饰）和朱迪（已故的琳恩·玛丽·斯图尔特饰），他最终会改变自己的态度。
约翰逊非常擅长扮演并不总是讨人喜欢的失败者。他的一位新朋友说她认为他看起来像蒂莫西·查拉梅 (Timothée Chalamet)；他笑道，以前从来没有人这么说过或这么想过。但她可能说得对：达斯蒂属于马蒂至尊谱系。
斯汀伯根也可爱又有趣，而哈里斯则是完美的老爸；难道就不能有更多的电影人和编剧想办法好好利用我们七十多岁的老人吗？
这部电影中也有很多喜剧演员扮演小角色：帕顿·奥斯瓦尔特是俱乐部的常驻泡菜球爱好者，克洛伊·法恩曼是一个疯狂的前女友，克里斯·帕内尔是一名网球职业选手。该片由乔什·格林鲍姆执导，肖恩·克莱门茨编剧，在愚蠢与真诚之间取得了一定的成功。而且，值得赞扬的是，它并没有低到让匹克球及其球员成为每个笑话的笑柄。当然，有一些；但大部分都是做得如此有爱。
在这一切之中，有一个序列可能是今年最有趣的部分。我不会残忍到破坏我正在谈论的内容（或者大胆地认为言语可能会公正地表达），但我会说它不涉及任何主要角色，并且与故事没有什么关系。这真是荒谬又完美。
格林鲍姆也是《倒钩和明星去维斯塔德尔玛》和即将上映的《太空球》续集的幕后制作人，他正在为自己作为可靠的喜剧导演开辟一个良好的空间，这种类型在大银幕上几乎濒临灭绝。
对于电影来说，这是一个奇怪的时刻：人们对院线电影的兴趣显然已经存在，而不仅仅是《奥德赛》。然而，本周有两部主流喜剧，包括凯文·哈特的单身派对电影《72 小时》，其明星阵容将直接进入流媒体。一线希望是，人们仍在制作直接喜剧，但当他们完全跳过电影院时，就会失去一些东西。当然，您可以将家人聚集在客厅，但并非所有家庭都具有相同类型的幽默。如果你周围的人不开心，你就很难屈服于某件事。在剧院里，至少有机会与其他志趣相投的人一起欢笑，甚至呻吟。（我无法评价《72小时》的质量。）
有些电影仍然有机会，比如《盖尔·道特里和名人性爱通行证》，但当大而愚蠢的喜剧不再是常规节目的一部分时，很难相信看电影会真正“回归”。
周五在 Apple TV 上播出的《丁克》因“粗俗语言和色情内容”被美国电影协会评为 PG-13。片长：102分钟。两星半（满分四星）。

item.title: 《哥斯拉》聋哑女演员凯莉·霍特尔 (Kaylee Hottle) 因车祸去世，年仅 18 岁
item.description:
弗雷德里克县治安官办公室称，因出演两部《哥斯拉》电影而闻名的聋哑女演员凯莉·霍特尔周二早些时候在马里兰州的一场车祸中丧生。他当时 18 岁。
凌晨 3 点前，汽车冲出道路，撞入伊贾姆斯维尔的一个涵洞，霍特尔是车上两名乘客之一。治安官办公室的一份声明称，司机被送往当地一家医院，伤势没有生命危险，另一名乘客拒绝接受医疗救治。治安官办公室表示，据信超速是造成这起事故的一个因素，目前事故正在调查中。
霍特尔是美国本土手语使用者，她的家人也是聋人。他的父母约书亚·霍特尔 (Joshua Hottle) 和凯西·霍特尔 (Ketsi Hottle) 在社交媒体上分享了有关他去世的感人视频。
“凯莉的母亲和她的四个兄弟姐妹悲痛欲绝。他们在家里，悲伤不已。我正在前往马里兰州的路上，将凯莉的遗体带回家，”父亲在机场用美国手语写道。
霍特尔的父亲说，霍特尔从事故现场被空运到医院之前，她的心脏停止了跳动。“非常感谢我们一起度过的 18 年，我希望我们能拥有更多，”他说。
约书亚·霍特尔 (Joshua Hottle) 还联系了司机，治安官办公室称司机是一名来自弗雷德里克 (Frederick) 的 19 岁男子。
“我已经原谅你了，不要让这件事毁了你的余生。”父亲说道。
“我仍然觉得凯莉和我们在一起，”这位母亲在帖子中用美国手语说道。
在《哥斯拉大战金刚》和续集《哥斯拉×金刚：新帝国》中，霍特尔扮演了一个名叫贾的孤儿女孩，她看穿了金刚凶恶的外表，并与怪物建立了特殊的联系，并用手语与他交流。
“听到这个消息我很伤心。我们会非常想念你的，凯莉，”她在《哥斯拉》中的搭档米莉·鲍比·布朗在 Instagram 上写道。
联合主演亚历山大·斯卡斯加德 (Alexander Skarsgård) 学习了美国手语与霍特尔交流，他在 2021 年告诉流行文化网站 Junkee，这位年轻女演员的专业精神和她快速将导演暗示融入表演的能力给他和其他人留下了深刻的印象。
在 2024 年接受美联社采访时，霍特尔通过翻译表示，她在出演《哥斯拉》之前从未看过怪物电影，因此她的父亲帮助她了解了这一类型。
“我钦佩很多人，但我钦佩的人，我主要钦佩的少数人，是我的父母和姐妹，”霍特尔说。
霍特尔解释说，当他拍摄第一部电影时，他有点担心之后是否能够回到“正常生活”。
“人们确实在学校认出了我，但这并不像我想象的那么糟糕，”霍特尔说。
霍特尔此前曾出演过 2021 年电视节目《Magnum P.I.》。他被列为即将上映的电影《杀不死我们》的演员之一。
“我心碎了。在这个难以想象的时刻，我的心与他的家人同在，”该片的搭档、聋人女演员阿拉夸·考克斯在 Instagram 上写道。
其他著名的聋人演员也在网上分享了悲伤的信息。
奥斯卡奖得主玛莉·马特林 (Marlee Matlin) 在 Instagram 上写道：“可爱的凯莉·霍特尔 (Kaylee Hottle) 的去世让我感到非常悲痛。愿她的美丽和才华被永远铭记。”
主演电影《尾声》的奥斯卡奖得主特洛伊·科瑟尔写道，霍特尔的去世让他想起了自己的女儿，并提醒他不要把时间视为理所当然。
科楚尔写道：“我无法用语言来表达今天早上发生的事情。”他向霍特尔一家表示“最深切的哀悼”。
奥斯汀德克萨斯聋人学校的官员也在脸书上宣布了霍特尔的去世，并写道将为他的同学和朋友提供咨询和支持服务。
学校写道：“当我们共同哀悼这一巨大损失时，请记住凯莉的亲人。” ___ 布恩从爱达荷州博伊西报道。拉格从密歇根州安娜堡报道。
这个故事是由美联社编辑在生成人工智能工具的帮助下从英文翻译而来。
